problem.title: Minimum Path Sum
problem.description: Given a m x n grid filled with non-negative numbers, find a path from top left to bottom right, which minimizes the sum of all numbers along its path. You can only move right or down.
assumptions: Return minimum path sum from top-left to bottom-right.

example.explanation: Path: 1→3→1→1→1 = 7. Only move right or down. DP: dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming
solutions.description: Build the solution in-place by transforming each cell to the minimum cumulative cost to reach it, first filling the top row and left column as forced paths, then using dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]) for the rest. The answer is the value at the bottom-right cell after the pass. The key insight is that since we can only move right or down, each cell's optimal cost depends only on the cell above and to the left.
